To bind many into one, The Arbiters demanded psychologically active rituals, exhausting dances and grueling ordeals that served as credible displays of faith. These costly signals ensured no hypocrite could hide, forging a communal loyalty stronger than blood. Commanded by these stern judges to be fruitful, the faithful multiplied, leading to intense intergroup competition. In the fires of conflict, tribes bound by the sternest gods and most rigorous laws moved with a terrifying, single purpose. The fragmented perished, while the victors expanded across the horizon. Witnessing this strength, neighboring bands copied the rituals and laws of the conquerors, seeking the same social glue. Over twelve millennia of cultural time, the incidental echoes of the mind were forged by the fires of war into the Great Law. What began as a mental glitch became the architect of empire, transforming wandering strangers into a unified, global civilization bound by the eternal, divine, and ever watchful gaze.
